title: Implement gdml_transform and world-frame transform chain (numpy/scipy)
description: Implement the coordinate transform utilities in wirecell/util/gdml.py based on the research in wcpy-8o7. Required functions: (1) gdml_transform(pos_xyz_mm, rot_xyz_deg) -> 4x4 ndarray: builds a local-to-world homogeneous matrix from a GDML physvol position and rotation. Uses scipy.spatial.transform.Rotation.from_euler('xyz', angles, degrees=True).inv() for the active rotation. (2) compose_transforms(*transforms) -> 4x4 ndarray: multiplies a sequence of 4x4 matrices parent-first. (3) apply_transform(T, point_local) -> point_world: applies a 4x4 homogeneous matrix to a 3-vector. Write tests FIRST (red/green): test_identity_transform (pos=0, rot=0 leaves point unchanged), test_pure_translation, test_single_axis_rotation (x=90 deg rotates Z->Y as expected), test_rUWireAboutX_direction (x=150 gives wire at 30 deg from Z), test_two_level_composition (translation then rotation). NOTE: GDML tube z attribute is FULL length; half_L = z/2. Tests should cover this.
close_reason: Implemented. gdml.py has gdml_transform, compose_transforms, apply_transform. 14 tests all passing.

title: Research: VD wire connectivity, head/tail convention, and segment rules
description: Research the VD-specific wire geometry conventions by examining protodunevd GDML files and existing VD wires files (from wire-cell-data or dunevd.py): (1) How are pairs of U wires connected across a pair of faces, etc for V (W are not connected).  (2) What is the nearness criterion for pairing wires across faces - 'same X, small gap in Y-Z along wire direction': what specific tolerance in cm? (3) What is the head/tail convention for VD wires - which end points toward the channel electronics? Is it related to which face is 'top' vs 'bottom' in the GDML? (4) How are segment numbers assigned within a connected pair? Produce written design notes via 'bd update --design=...' with enough specificity to implement T5.3. After completing this, create new beads tasks for the VD connectivity implementation.
design:
VD wire connectivity conventions (from analysis of protodunevd-wires-larsoft-v3.json.bz2 and dunevd.py):

PLANES THAT ARE CROSS-FACE CONNECTED:
U (ident=0) and V (ident=1) induction planes only. W (ident=2, collection) wires are NEVER connected across faces; all W wires have segment=0 only. For anode 0 in the reference JSON: 97 cross-face channels per U and V plane out of ~287 wires per face per plane.

FACE BOUNDARY AND PAIRING CRITERION:
The two faces of a VD anode (open-book: side-by-side in Y, coplanar in X drift) meet at a shared boundary plane. At this boundary, the endpoint of a wire segment from face A exactly coincides in world-frame coordinates with one endpoint of the connecting wire segment from face B (same X, same Y, same Z to floating-point precision in the JSON). 
Implementation rule: two wire segments (one from each face of the same anode) are connected if they share an endpoint within ε = 1 mm in world frame. This is well below the wire pitch (4.76 mm). The boundary Y-position is implicitly defined by the TPC volume extents.
Geometric detail: the two faces have OPPOSITE Z-components of their wire direction due to rPlus180AboutY rotation of the Bot TPC. U wires in face0 (Top, identity rotation) run at +60 deg from Y toward +Z; U wires in face1 (Bot, rPlus180AboutY) run at +60 deg from Y toward -Z. They meet in a V-shape at the face boundary.

HEAD/TAIL CONVENTION FOR VD:
head = endpoint at higher Y_world (physically upward in GDML world frame). This is consistent with dunevd.py comment 'always point upward in Y'. In the reference JSON all wire segments (both seg=0 and seg=1 of cross-face wires, and standalone wires) have head.y > tail.y. For GDML-based extraction: after computing world-frame endpoints from the full transform chain, set head = endpoint with higher Y_GDML.

SEGMENT NUMBER ASSIGNMENT:
- Standalone wire (on one face only): segment=0.
- Cross-face connected wire pair: assign segment=0 to the segment on the face with HIGHER face.ident (= face1, Bot TPC, lower Y range); assign segment=1 to the segment on face with LOWER face.ident (= face0, Top TPC, higher Y range). Rule: seg = 1 - face.ident for ident in {0,1}.
- The lengths of seg=0 and seg=1 can vary widely (seg=0 can be shorter or longer than seg=1 depending on wire position). The assignment is purely face-ident-based, not length-based.
- Both seg=0 and seg=1 point head-upward (+Y) independently.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE FOR wcpy-nqh:
After extracting world-frame wire endpoints from GDML (one set per TPC/face), group faces into anode pairs. For each anode's U and V planes, run an O(N) spatial matching: build a dict keyed by (round(x,0), round(y,0), round(z,0)) mm from all endpoints, then for each wire's endpoints look up matches in the other face's endpoint dict. Match threshold: < 1 mm. Channel numbering: the face-1 (Bot) wire's segment gets channel N; the face-0 stub shares channel N. W planes: skip cross-face matching entirely.
close_reason: Research complete. Design notes written to issue. Two follow-up implementation tasks created: wcpy-bs5 (endpoint-matching) and wcpy-r38 (channel/segment assignment). Both block wcpy-nqh.

title: Group wires into planes, planes into faces using volume classifier
description: Implement build_detector_faces(vol_tree, wires_list, patterns) that groups WireGeom objects into PlaneGeom and FaceGeom using the role classifier (T1.5). Traversal: for each wire, walk up its parent chain in the GDML tree, finding the first ancestor with role 'plane' and the first with role 'face'. Group wires by their plane ancestor, planes by their face ancestor. Result: a list of FaceGeom objects (anodes not yet determined at this stage). Write tests: on the synthetic GDML, verify the expected number of faces, planes per face, and wires per plane.

title: Extract wire endpoints from GDML physvol tree in world frame
description: Implement extract_wires(vol_tree, defines, solids, root_vol, transform_fn) that traverses the GDML volume tree from root_vol and produces a list of WireGeom objects with world-frame tail/head endpoints. For each wire physvol: (1) accumulate transforms from root down to the wire physvol, (2) apply the final transform to the center ± (half_z * wire_direction) to get tail/head endpoints, (3) wire direction is the Z-axis of the local wire frame rotated to world frame. The transform_fn signature: transform_fn(local_point, transform_chain) -> world_point is supplied by the coordinate transform system (E2/T2.1). Write tests first on the synthetic GDML: verify wire endpoint coordinates against manually computed expected values.

title: Define intermediate geometry data classes (WireGeom, PlaneGeom, etc.)
description: Define Python dataclasses in wirecell/util/gdml.py (or a sub-module) for intermediate geometry: WireGeom(name: str, tail: np.ndarray, head: np.ndarray, radius: float, plane_name: str), PlaneGeom(name: str, wires: list[WireGeom]), FaceGeom(name: str, planes: list[PlaneGeom]), AnodeGeom(faces: list[FaceGeom]), DetectorGeom(anodes: list[AnodeGeom]). Note: tail/head will be 3-vectors in world frame (None until transforms are implemented). Write tests first: construct instances from synthetic data, verify attribute access, verify that WireGeom with tail=None does not crash on construction (transform is deferred).
close_reason: Dataclasses WireGeom, PlaneGeom, FaceGeom, AnodeGeom, DetectorGeom added to gdml.py; 9 tests all passing

title: Define and implement detector config JSON schema
description: Define the JSON schema for a detector configuration and implement load_config(path_or_name) in wirecell/util/gdml.py. The config JSON must include: 'role_patterns' (dict of role->regex string for wire/plane/face/detector), 'connectivity_mode' ('vd' or 'hd'), 'nearness_tolerance' (float in cm). load_config: if argument is an existing file path, load it as JSON; if it matches a known name, return the corresponding built-in dict. Validate required keys on load. Write tests first: (1) load a hand-crafted JSON config file, verify all keys parse correctly; (2) request an unknown named config, expect a clear error; (3) load a config with a missing required key, expect a validation error.
close_reason: load_config() implemented in gdml.py: file path or built-in name, validates required keys (role_patterns, connectivity_mode, nearness_tolerance). BUILTIN_CONFIGS dict ready for wcpy-8z5. 15 tests all passing.

title: Research: coordinate transform approaches for GDML hierarchy
description: Research the best approach for computing world-frame coordinates of wires from the GDML volume hierarchy. Must determine: (1) GDML rotation convention: are x/y/z rotation attributes applied as Rx*Ry*Rz (intrinsic) or Rz*Ry*Rx (extrinsic)? Verify against a known GDML example. (2) Evaluate Python packages: pyg4ometry (https://pyg4ometry.readthedocs.io), Geant4 Python bindings, others. For each: installation complexity, ability to read GDML and query world-frame positions, correctness. (3) Evaluate custom numpy approach: compose 4x4 homogeneous transforms through the tree. Produce written design notes in issue via 'bd update T21 --design=...' covering: chosen approach with justification, GDML rotation convention, example code snippet for a two-level transform. After completing this research, create new beads tasks for the implementation.
close_reason: Research complete. Convention confirmed: extrinsic XYZ passive rotation, invert for active. Use scipy+numpy custom approach. Design notes recorded.

title: Classify GDML volumes by role using name patterns
description: Implement match_role(name, patterns) and classify_volumes(vol_names, patterns) in wirecell/util/gdml.py. The patterns dict maps role names (e.g. 'wire', 'plane', 'face', 'detector') to compiled regex patterns. match_role returns the first matching role or None. classify_volumes returns {role: [vol_names]}. Must be callable with patterns from the detector config system (E3). Write tests first: given a synthetic volume name list and a hand-crafted patterns dict, verify each volume gets the expected role. Test that a volume matching no pattern returns None (not an error).

title: Parse GDML <structure> section into logical/physvol tree
description: Implement parse_structure(gdml_root, defines, solids) in wirecell/util/gdml.py that builds a dict mapping logical volume names to their properties and children. For each <volume>: record name, solidref. For each <physvol> child: record volumeref, position (inline or via positionref lookup in defines), rotation (inline or via rotationref lookup in defines). If physvol lacks a name attribute, use the child volume name as the physvol name. Result: {vol_name: {'solid': str, 'physvols': [{'vol': str, 'pos': (x,y,z), 'rot': (rx,ry,rz)}]}}. Write tests first: traverse from the TPC volume to individual wire volumes and verify the chain is correct.

title: Parse GDML <solids> section for tube geometry
description: Implement parse_solids(gdml_root) in wirecell/util/gdml.py that returns a dict mapping solid name -> tube dimensions for all <tube> elements. Extract: rmax (outer radius), z (half-length per GDML convention), lunit. Convert to WCT mm. Return: {name: {'rmax': float, 'half_z': float}} (using half_z consistently, converting if needed). Write tests first (red): load synthetic fixture, assert tube dimensions for each wire solid match the hand-crafted expected values.

title: Create minimal synthetic test GDML fixture
description: Create a minimal hand-crafted GDML file for unit testing. Must include: one U-plane, one V-plane, one W-plane each with ~5 wires, one logical TPC volume containing the three planes, wire physvols using both inline positions (<position name=... x=... .../> directly in physvol) and reference-style (<positionref ref=.../>) to cover both GDML styles, at least one physvol without a name attribute. Commit to wirecell/util/test/data/minimal.gdml or similar. Add a pytest fixture that returns the path to this file. Write red test first: test_synthetic_gdml_exists() should pass when the file is committed. This task delivers the test infrastructure that all subsequent GDML parsing tests depend on.
close_reason: minimal.gdml created in wirecell/util/test/data/; conftest.py adds minimal_gdml_path fixture; 13 tests cover XML validity, all required sections, both position styles, anonymous physvols, and wire/plane counts.

title: Parse GDML <define> section (positions and rotations)
description: Implement parse_define(gdml_root) in wirecell/util/gdml.py that returns a dict of all named positions and rotations from the GDML <define> section. Positions: extract x, y, z attributes and unit, convert to WCT mm. Rotations: extract x, y, z Euler angle attributes and unit (deg or rad), convert to radians. Result: {'positions': {name: (x,y,z)}, 'rotations': {name: (rx,ry,rz)}}. Write tests first (red): load the synthetic GDML fixture, assert specific named positions and rotations appear with correct values (verify unit conversion).

title: Epic: Intermediate geometry data structures (GDML -> WireGeom bridge)
description: Define and implement the intermediate data structures that bridge the GDML volume tree and the wires schema. These capture: wire geometry (endpoints in world frame, rotation, radius), plane membership, face membership, and anode grouping. Algorithms for volume classification, connectivity, ordering, and schema population all operate on these structures. Must also implement extraction from GDML tree and face-to-anode geometric pairing.

title: Epic: Detector config system (JSON and named configs)
description: Implement a detector configuration system that maps detector names to parameter sets governing the gdml-to-wires conversion. A config specifies: (1) regex patterns for each wires-schema role (wire, plane, face/TPC, detector/cryostat), (2) connectivity mode (vd or hd), (3) nearness tolerance. Config can be loaded from a JSON file or looked up by canonical detector name (e.g. pdvd) from a built-in dict. CLI usage: --detector pdvd or --detector /path/to/config.json.

title: Epic: Coordinate transform system for GDML world-frame
description: Research and implement a system for computing wire endpoint positions in GDML world frame by composing rotation and translation transforms through the full volume hierarchy (world -> cryostat -> TPC -> plane -> wire physvol). GDML uses an Euler rotation convention (rotation_x then rotation_y then rotation_z) that must be verified. Options to evaluate: custom numpy-based transform chain, pyg4ometry package, Geant4 Python bindings. This gates all world-frame coordinate work. A research task (T2.1) must be done before implementation tasks can be planned.

title: Epic: GDML XML parsing core
description: Implement the core GDML XML parser that builds a Python representation of a GDML file. Must handle: (1) <define> section for named positions and rotations, (2) <solids> section for tube geometries, (3) <structure> section for logical volumes and physvol containment tree. Must handle physvol without a name attribute (take child volume name). Must handle both inline and ref-style position/rotation. This is the foundation all other work depends on. All values must be converted to WCT units (mm, radians).

title: VD connectivity: channel and segment number assignment from matched wire pairs
description: After the endpoint-matching step (wcpy-bs5) produces connected wire pairs, assign channel and segment numbers. Rules from research (wcpy-zv1): standalone wires (no cross-face match) get segment=0 and a unique channel; cross-face connected pairs share the same channel, with segment=0 assigned to the face1 (Bot, higher face.ident) wire and segment=1 to the face0 (Top, lower face.ident) wire. Channel numbers must be globally unique across all anodes. This step populates the WireGeom.channel and WireGeom.segment fields.
close_reason: assign_vd_channels implemented: adds channel/segment fields to WireGeom, assigns shared channel to cross-face pairs (seg=0 on face1, seg=1 on face0) and unique channel+seg=0 to standalone wires. Returns next available channel for global uniqueness. 13 tests all passing.

title: VD connectivity: endpoint-matching algorithm for cross-face U/V wire pairing
description: Implement the endpoint-matching algorithm that identifies which U and V wire segments from adjacent faces of a VD anode should be connected. After extracting world-frame wire endpoints (from GDML transform chain), for each anode pair: build a spatial lookup dict keyed by (round(x), round(y), round(z)) mm from all wire endpoints in face0, then for each wire endpoint in face1's U and V planes look for a match within 1 mm. This identifies cross-face connected pairs. W plane wires are skipped. Returns a list of (face0_wire, face1_wire) pairs for subsequent channel/segment assignment.
close_reason: find_cross_face_pairs implemented in gdml.py with _match_plane_wires and _is_collection_plane helpers. 11 tests all passing.

title: Integration test: protodunevd_v4 gdml-to-wires vs known-good wires file
description: Write an integration test that runs gdml-to-wires on protodunevd_v4_refactored.gdml with the built-in pdvd_v4 config and compares the output against a known-good VD wires JSON file. Test should check: (1) number of detectors, anodes, faces, planes matches expected, (2) wire count per plane matches known-good counts, (3) channel ID ranges match (min/max per plane), (4) a sampled set of wire endpoint coordinates match within a tolerance of ~0.1 mm. Document where the known-good wires file is obtained (wire-cell-data repo or similar). This test may be marked as slow/integration and skipped in fast CI runs. After this test passes, gdml-to-wires is considered validated for protodunevd_v4.

title: Implement wirecell/util/gdml.py module and gdml-to-wires CLI command
description: Implement the top-level convert(gdml_path, config) -> schema.Store function in wirecell/util/gdml.py that orchestrates the full pipeline: parse_define, parse_solids, parse_structure, extract_wires, build_detector_faces, pair_faces_into_anodes, find_connected_pairs, assign_channels, sort_wires_by_pitch, sort_planes_by_drift, build_store. Add the gdml-to-wires Click command to wirecell/util/__main__.py: takes --detector (name or JSON file path), optional --output (default: input.json replacing .gdml), and positional input GDML file. Write output via persist.dump() supporting .json and .json.bz2. Add the command to the util CLI group. Write a smoke test: run gdml-to-wires on the synthetic test GDML, verify output is valid JSON with at least one detector, anode, face, plane, and wire.

title: Build schema.Store from intermediate geometry and channel assignments
description: Implement build_store(detector_geom, channel_map) -> schema.Store in wirecell/util/gdml.py. channel_map: {wire_name: (channel_id, segment)}. Must: (1) create schema.Point for all wire tail/head endpoints (deduplicating shared points), (2) create schema.Wire(ident=wire_in_plane_index, channel=channel_id, segment=seg, tail=point_idx, head=point_idx) using the head/tail convention (head = toward electronics = segment=0 end or the end of segment=N toward segment=N-1), (3) create schema.Plane(ident=U/V/W index, wires=[sorted indices]), (4) create schema.Face(ident=0 or 1, planes=[plane indices in U/V/W order]), (5) create schema.Anode(ident=anode_index, faces=[face indices]), (6) create schema.Detector(ident=0, anodes=[...]), (7) assemble schema.Store. Write tests first on small synthetic data: verify all indices are in-range, counts match, round-trip through persist.todict/fromdict.

title: Implement wire ordering by pitch and plane ordering by drift (U/V/W)
description: Implement two ordering functions in wirecell/util/gdml.py: (1) sort_wires_by_pitch(plane_geom) -> ordered list of WireGeom. Pitch direction = direction perpendicular to the wire direction within the plane normal. Wire midpoints projected onto the pitch direction give the sort key. The sort must respect the schema convention: cross product of wire direction and pitch direction gives a direction anti-parallel to drift. (2) sort_planes_by_drift(face_geom) -> ordered list of PlaneGeom in U/V/W order. The first plane in the drift direction = U (plane.ident=0), second = V (1), third = W (2). Drift direction is inferred from the face geometry (normal to the plane face, pointing toward the cathode based on geometry). Write tests first: verify ordering on synthetic data with known wire positions.

title: Implement VD channel ID assignment
description: Implement assign_vd_channels(detector_geom, connectivity) that assigns globally unique channel IDs following the convention determined in T5.2 research. Starting from the first anode, iterate in detector-defined order, iterating planes in U/V/W order, iterating wires in pitch order; for segment=0 wires assign the next channel ID and for their segment=1 partners assign the same channel ID. Third plane wires get unique IDs similarly. Result: {wire_name: channel_id}. Write a test that compares a sample of channel IDs against values from an existing known-good VD wires JSON file.

title: Implement VD wire connectivity and segment assignment
description: Implement find_vd_connected_pairs(anode_geom, nearness_tolerance) using the conventions determined in T5.1 research. For each anode: (1) identify which two planes have cross-face jumpers, (2) for each wire in plane A of face 0, find the wire in plane A of face 1 whose endpoint is within nearness_tolerance of an endpoint of the first wire, (3) assign segment=0 to the wire directly connected to electronics (determined by head/tail convention from T5.1 research), segment=1 to its connected partner. Third plane wires get segment=0 with no partner. Result: {wire_name: {'segment': int, 'connected_to': wire_name or None}}. Write tests first using synthetic two-face VD GDML: verify correct pairing and segment assignments.

title: Research: VD channel ID numbering convention
description: Research how channel IDs are numbered in the VD design. Examine: (1) the existing dunevd.py text-file-based loader for any channel numbering hints, (2) the protodunevd GDML files, (3) wire-cell-data VD wires JSON files if available, (4) DUNE electronics documentation if accessible. Determine: starting channel ID for each CRU, ordering of planes within a CRU for channel numbering, ordering of CRUs across the detector. Produce a written specification in the issue via 'bd update --design=...' sufficient to implement assign_vd_channels(). After completing this, create a new beads task for the VD channel assignment implementation (T5.4).

title: Implement face-to-anode geometric pairing (VD and HD modes)
description: Implement pair_faces_into_anodes(faces, connectivity_mode) that groups pairs of FaceGeom into AnodeGeom based on geometric rules. VD mode: faces are co-planar and side-by-side ('open book') - find pairs with the same drift-normal (X) coordinate and adjacent positions in the plane perpendicular to X. HD mode: faces are parallel and opposite ('closed book') - find pairs with coincident Y-Z extents but opposite X positions. Each mode must raise an error if an odd number of faces is found (no unpaired faces). Write tests: two synthetic faces placed as VD co-planar pair correctly form one anode; two faces placed as HD opposite pair correctly form one anode.

title: Add built-in configs for protodunevd_v4 and protodunevd_v5
description: Add built-in detector configs for protodunevd_v4_refactored.gdml and protodunevd_v5_ggd.gdml as Python dicts in wirecell/util/gdml.py. Inspect both files to determine the name regex patterns for each role: wire volumes (e.g. r'volTPCWire.*'), plane volumes (e.g. r'volTPCPlane.*'), TPC/face volumes (e.g. r'volTPC\d+'), cryostat/detector volume (r'volCryostat'). Note that v4 uses volTPC0/volTPC1/etc while v5 uses volTPC_0/volTPC_1/etc - they may share one config using a sufficiently general regex or need separate configs. Write tests that run classify_volumes on a small sample of real volume names from each file and verify classification is correct.

title: Epic: Wires schema population from intermediate geometry
description: Implement conversion of the intermediate DetectorGeom (with connectivity and channel assignments) to a schema.Store object ready for serialization. Must implement: (1) wire ordering within plane by pitch direction, (2) plane ordering within face by drift position (U/V/W), plane.ident: U=0, V=1, W=2, (3) wire.ident as wire-in-plane index, (4) build schema.Store with all flat arrays and hierarchy indices. Output must be identical to what persist.dump/load would round-trip.

title: Epic: gdml-to-wires CLI command and integration tests
description: Implement wirecell/util/gdml.py as the main conversion module and the gdml-to-wires CLI command in wirecell/util/__main__.py. Command signature: wcpy util gdml-to-wires --detector pdvd [--output wires.json] input.gdml. Add integration tests against protodunevd_v4 and v5 GDML files comparing output to known-good wires files. Also support gegede-generated test GDML and downloaded production GDML in tests.

title: Epic: VD wire connectivity and channel assignment
description: Research and implement VD-specific algorithms for: (1) wire segment connectivity (pairs of wires in adjacent co-planar faces connected by jumpers across the anode 'spine'), (2) wire head/tail orientation (which endpoint is toward the electronics), (3) globally-unique channel ID assignment. In VD, 2 of 3 planes have cross-face jumpers (segment 0 directly connected to electronics, segment 1 on the far side), 1 plane is one-to-one. Begins with research tasks (T5.1, T5.2) that produce sub-plans for implementation.

title: Integration test: protodunevd_v5 support and validation
description: Add support for protodunevd_v5_ggd.gdml (different naming conventions from v4: volTPC_0 vs volTPC0 etc.) by creating a built-in pdvd_v5 config with appropriate name patterns. Write an integration test comparing gdml-to-wires output on v5 against a known-good wires file. If v4 and v5 share sufficiently similar patterns, a single 'pdvd' config using a broader regex (e.g. r'volTPC\w+' for faces) may cover both - determine this during implementation. After passing, document which gdml files are supported by which named configs.

title: Research: HD wire connectivity, head/tail convention, and segment rules
description: Research HD-specific wire geometry conventions: (1) Which two planes wrap (first two = U+V? confirmed or check), and which does not (W = collection plane)? (2) What is the nearness criterion for HD wrapping - 'same Y-Z, offset in X': what X tolerance? (3) Head/tail convention: top sub-detector wires point generally upward (+Y), bottom sub-detector wires point generally downward (-Y) - confirm this from GDML/existing files. (4) How many segments can a channel have (up to 3?) and how are they numbered? Produce written design notes via 'bd update --design=...' sufficient to implement HD connectivity. After completing, create new beads tasks for HD implementation.

title: Research: HD channel ID numbering convention
description: Research how channel IDs are numbered in the HD design (protoDUNE-HD, DUNE FD HD). Examine existing HD wires files (from wire-cell-data), the multitpc.py loader, and any DUNE electronics documentation. Determine: channel ID range per APA, ordering of planes, ordering of APAs, handling of wrapped wire segments (which segment gets the base channel ID). Produce a specification via 'bd update --design=...' sufficient to implement assign_hd_channels(). After completing, create a new beads task for HD channel assignment implementation.

title: Epic: HD wire connectivity and channel assignment
description: Research and implement HD-specific algorithms for wire wrapping connectivity and channel ID assignment. In HD, the first two wire planes in each face have wires that wrap around to the opposite parallel face (like a closed book). Up to 2-3 wire segments share one channel. The third plane does not wrap. Begins with research tasks (T6.1, T6.2). HD should be implemented after VD is complete and validated.

title: TEST: delete me
description: testing bd create output format

value: This repo (wire-cell-python) is adding a gdml-to-wires feature: convert GDML XML detector geometry files to the Wire-Cell 'wires schema' JSON (wirecell/util/wires/schema.py). The new code lives in wirecell/util/gdml.py and a CLI command 'gdml-to-wires' is added to wirecell/util/__main__.py. Target detectors: protodunevd_v4, protodunevd_v5 (VD) and protodune-HD (HD). The full plan is in beads: run 'bd list --status=open' and 'bd ready' to see tasks.

Wire-Cell wires schema summary (wirecell/util/wires/schema.py):
Hierarchy (trunk to leaf): Store -> Detector -> Anode -> Face -> Plane -> Wire -> Point.
Children are referenced by INDEX into flat arrays: Store holds flat lists of each type.
- Point(x, y, z): world-frame coordinates in WCT units (mm base unit)
- Wire(ident, channel, segment, tail, head): tail/head are Point indices. head = toward electronics.
- Plane(ident, wires): wires = list of Wire indices in INCREASING PITCH order. ident = 0/1/2 for U/V/W.
- Face(ident, planes): planes list in U/V/W order.
- Anode(ident, faces): faces list.
- Detector(ident, anodes): anodes list.
- Store(detectors, anodes, faces, planes, wires, points): all flat lists.
Persist: wirecell/util/wires/persist.py — load(name) and dump(filename, store) handle .json and .json.bz2.

GDML file locations for testing (never read large files in full; grep/head only):
- protodunevd_v4: /home/bviren/dev/gdml-to-wires/dunecore/dunecore/Geometry/gdml/protodunevd_v4_refactored.gdml (43k lines)
- protodunevd_v5: /home/bviren/dev/gdml-to-wires/dunecore/dunecore/Geometry/gdml/protodunevd_v5_ggd.gdml (38k lines)
- gegede package (for test GDML generation): /home/bviren/dev/gdml-to-wires/gegede/

Volume naming v4: volTPCWireU0 (wire), volTPCPlaneU0 (plane), volTPC0 (face/TPC), volCryostat (detector).
Volume naming v5: volTPCWireU0_0 (wire), volTPCPlaneU_0 (plane), volTPC_0 (face), volCryostat (detector).
Each logical TPC volume (e.g. volTPC_0) is placed TWICE in the cryostat: once at 'Top' position (identity rotation) and once at 'Bot' position (rPlus180AboutY). These two placements = one anode.

Key architectural decisions for gdml-to-wires (from planning session):
1. COORDINATES: wire endpoints must be in GDML world frame (full transform chain required).
2. FACE-TO-ANODE PAIRING: VD = 'open book' (two faces co-planar, side-by-side, same X/drift coordinate); HD = 'closed book' (two faces parallel and opposite, same Y-Z extents but opposite X).
3. PLANE ORDERING: U/V/W identified by drift position — first plane in drift direction = U (ident=0), then V (1), then W (2). plane.ident is 0/1/2, NOT the full wire_plane_id.
4. CHANNEL IDs: globally unique across detector, detector-specific numbering. VD: 2 of 3 planes have cross-face jumpers (seg 0 + seg 1 share a channel); 3rd plane is 1-to-1. HD: wires wrap across faces, up to 3 segments. Channel numbering needs research tasks (wcpy-yzh, wcpy-6dd).
5. HEAD/TAIL: head = toward electronics. HD top sub-detector: generally upward (+Y). HD bottom: generally downward (-Y). VD: needs research (wcpy-zv1).
6. CONFIG: JSON file or named string (e.g. --detector pdvd or --detector my.json). Built-in dict in gdml.py for known detectors.

GDML rotation convention (confirmed by research, wcpy-8o7): <rotation x=a y=b z=c unit=deg/> stores a PASSIVE rotation M = Mz(c)*My(b)*Mx(a) (extrinsic XYZ order). The ACTIVE local-to-world rotation is M^{-1} = M^T. In code: R = Rotation.from_euler('xyz', [a,b,c], degrees=True).inv().as_matrix() — use lowercase 'xyz' (extrinsic), the .inv() is MANDATORY. GDML tube z attribute = FULL length; half_L = z_gdml/2. Wire axis is local Z; endpoints: tail=[0,0,-half_L], head=[0,0,+half_L]. Composition: T_world = T_cryo @ T_tpc @ T_plane @ T_wire (parent first, child last).

What is already implemented in wirecell/util/gdml.py (as of first commits):
- gdml_transform(pos_xyz_mm, rot_xyz_deg) -> 4x4 ndarray: builds local-to-world homogeneous matrix
- compose_transforms(*Ts) -> 4x4 ndarray: composes parent-first chain
- apply_transform(T, point) -> 3-vector: applies 4x4 matrix to a point
Tests: wirecell/util/test/test_gdml_transform.py (14 tests, all passing).
NOT yet implemented: GDML XML parsing, volume classification, detector config system, connectivity, channel assignment, schema population, CLI command. See 'bd ready' for next available tasks.
